# Test: Complex match guards with class hierarchies, enums, and properties
# Guards evaluate expressions involving method calls, boolean logic, and state checks

enum Priority:
    LOW = 1
    MEDIUM = 2
    HIGH = 3

class Task:
    _name: str
    priority: Priority
    _completed: bool

    def __init__(self, name: str, priority: Priority):
        self._name = name
        self.priority = priority
        self._completed = False

    property get name(self) -> str:
        return self._name

    property get is_completed(self) -> bool:
        return self._completed

    def mark_complete(self) -> None:
        self._completed = True

    def mark_incomplete(self) -> None:
        self._completed = False

    @virtual
    def get_effort(self) -> int:
        return 1

class ComplexTask(Task):
    subtasks: list[str]

    def __init__(self, name: str, priority: Priority):
        super().__init__(name, priority)
        self.subtasks = []

    def add_subtask(self, subtask: str) -> None:
        self.subtasks.append(subtask)

    @override
    def get_effort(self) -> int:
        return 1 + len(self.subtasks)

def should_escalate(t: Task) -> bool:
    return t.priority == Priority.MEDIUM and not t.is_completed

def classify_task(t: Task) -> str:
    # Get values once to avoid repeated calculations
    effort: int = t.get_effort()
    priority: Priority = t.priority
    is_done: bool = t.is_completed
    
    match t:
        case _ if is_done and effort > 5:
            return "completed_heavy"
        case _ if is_done:
            return "completed_simple"
        case _ if should_escalate(t):
            # Escalate MEDIUM incomplete tasks
            return "escalate"
        case _ if priority == Priority.HIGH and effort > 3:
            return "high_complex"
        case _ if priority == Priority.HIGH:
            return "high_simple"
        case _ if effort >= 4:
            return "heavy_work"
        case _ if priority == Priority.LOW:
            return "low_priority"
        case _:
            return "standard"

def main():
    # Simple tasks
    simple_high = Task("Simple API", Priority.HIGH)
    simple_med = Task("Review docs", Priority.MEDIUM)
    simple_low = Task("Tidy up", Priority.LOW)
    simple_low.mark_complete()

    # Complex tasks
    complex_high = ComplexTask("Rewrite engine", Priority.HIGH)
    complex_high.add_subtask("Plan")
    complex_high.add_subtask("Code")
    complex_high.add_subtask("Test")

    print(classify_task(simple_high))
    print(classify_task(simple_med))
    print(classify_task(complex_high))

    # Complete and reclassify
    complex_high.mark_complete()
    print(classify_task(complex_high))

    # Make it heavy work again
    complex_high.mark_incomplete()
    complex_high.add_subtask("Deploy")
    complex_high.add_subtask("Monitor")
    print(classify_task(complex_high))

    print(classify_task(simple_low))

    # Medium task with enough work to matter
    complex_med = ComplexTask("Refactor module", Priority.MEDIUM)
    complex_med.add_subtask("Audit")
    complex_med.add_subtask("Migrate")
    complex_med.add_subtask("Verify")
    print(classify_task(complex_med))
expected_output:
high_simple
escalate
high_complex
completed_simple
high_complex
completed_simple
escalate
actual_output:
high_simple
escalate
high_complex
completed_simple
high_complex
completed_simple
escalate
